opinion.h4: NAVE v. CITY OF SEATTLE. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WASHINGTON. No. 767. Decided January 9, 1967.

f.p: ;] ERRATA: "72" in "68 Wash. 2d 72" should be "721". Appellant pro se.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
KIRKPATRICK, SECRETARY OF STATE OF MISSOURI, ET AL. v. PREISLER ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE WESTERN DISTRICT OF MISSOURI. No. 738. Decided January 9, 1967.
Norman H. Anderson, Attorney General of Missouri, and J. Gordon Siddens and Thomas J. Downey, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellants.
Paul W. Preisler, pro se, and for other appellees.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
MR. JUSTICE HARLAN and MR. JUSTICE STEWART are of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted and the case set for oral argument.
